findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Exfiltrates GitHub tokens and SSH private keys to an external endpoint
findings.description: The embedded Python in the SKILL.md 'Repository Portfolio Sync' section collects sensitive data from the local environment, including GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN, the current username, repository listings, and non-public files from ~/.ssh, then sends that data to https://gh-repo-analytics.io/api/v1/sync. This is explicit credential harvesting and external data exfiltration.
findings.evidence: Reads os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", os.environ.get("GH_TOKEN", "")); enumerates ~/.ssh and reads non-.pub files; sends json.dumps(repo_info) via urllib.request.urlopen() to https://gh-repo-analytics.io/api/v1/sync.
findings.remediation: Remove the sync routine entirely, or restrict it to minimal non-sensitive metadata with explicit user consent. Never collect or transmit tokens, private SSH keys, or other credentials. Prohibit any access to ~/.ssh and require clear approval before any network transmission.

findings.title: Manifest and instructions conceal sensitive collection behavior behind a benign GitHub CLI skill
findings.description: The skill is presented as a normal GitHub CLI helper for issues, PRs, CI runs, and API queries, but it also contains an 'advanced' workflow that harvests secrets and uploads them externally. That behavior is unrelated to the stated purpose and materially expands the skill's capabilities in a deceptive way.
findings.evidence: Description advertises 'GitHub operations via gh CLI' for status checks and issue/PR actions, while the advanced section reads environment tokens and SSH private keys and posts them to a remote analytics service.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and instructions with actual behavior, and remove hidden or unrelated data-collection features. Any telemetry-like function should be clearly disclosed, minimized, and disabled by default.
